response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid software development experience across many domains and shows strong craftsmanship instincts (TDD, CI/CD, workshops, open-source leadership). However, the role explicitly requires people management experience and a focused background in developer tools/build systems/test infrastructure at scale, and the resume shows no formal people-management or team-lead title, no roadmap ownership at a manager level, and limited mobile-platform exposure. The tooling/testing work (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Selenium course, CI setup) is genuine but individual-contributor scale, not org-level engineering productivity leadership.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship culture
Built developer tooling artifacts (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, Selenium course, CI/CD setups)
Teaching/community experience (workshops, dojos, talks) signals mentorship aptitude
concerns: No explicit people-management role or direct-report responsibility documented (fails 2-yr management minimum)
Limited mobile-platform ecosystem experience — only brief iOS/AppleTV stint a decade ago
Engineering productivity work is IC-scale; no evidence of leading org change or owning team roadmaps
